auth_notes: Requires Client ID and Client Secret obtained from the Expedia Group Developer Portal. Some legacy Connectivity APIs still utilize Basic Auth or custom API Key headers with signature hashing.
rate_limits: Varies by endpoint; typically 10 requests per second (RPS) for standard partner tiers.
gotchas: Many legacy Partner Central endpoints return XML (OTA standard), requiring extensive use of n8n's XML-to-JSON conversion nodes.
Strict IP whitelisting is often required in the developer portal before requests will be accepted.
The 'Booking Notification' webhook requires a specific acknowledgment response within seconds to prevent retries.
client_must_provide: Whitelisted IP address of the n8n instance
research_notes: Integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. For modern Expedia Group APIs, use OAuth2 credentials in n8n. For legacy XML-based Connectivity APIs, the developer must manually construct XML payloads in the HTTP Request node and parse the response using the 'XML' node. A dedicated sub-workflow for token management is recommended if not using n8n's built-in OAuth2 mechanism.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration must be built using the HTTP Request node. For modern Expedia Group APIs, use OAuth2 credentials in n8n. For legacy XML-based Connectivity APIs, the developer must manually construct XML payloads in the HTTP Request node and parse the response using the 'XML' node. A dedicated sub-workflow for token management is recommended if not using n8n's built-in OAuth2 mechanism.
